requirementSignalSummary.requirementText: The `%` branch of `resolveWorkers` skipped the NaN validation the numeric branch performs, so `--workers=abc%` (or an unset env var expanding to `--workers=%`)
The dispatcher then allocated zero worker slots, ran no tests, and exited 0 — silent success while nothing ran
Apply the same validation to the percentage branch and add a regression test
Fixes https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/issues/41679

requirementSignalSummary.requirementText: Linked issue axios/axios#6878: onDownloadProgress occurred after request solved
### Describe the bug
env: node v22
Starting from axios 1.7.3 (https://github.com/axios/axios/pull/6518), in node environment `onDownloadProgress` maybe invoke after request solved
It may be affected by the throttling method
Although the last task is finally executed through flush function, the execution order of queueMicroTask is later than response.data.on('close', callback)
### To Reproduce
If the frequency of receiving file stream chunks is bigger than 3, this phenomenon will be triggered

requirementSignalSummary.requirementText: Linked issue pytest-dev/pytest#14637: assertion diff: trailing character skip fails when first characters differ (off-by-one in `left[-i]` when `i=0`)
Was looking at assertion diff output and noticed the trailing skip optimization wasn't kicking in when the compared strings differed at the start
Traced it to `_diff_text` in `compare_text.py` line 62:
`-0 == 0` in Python, so `left[-0]` is actually `left[0]` — the first character, not the last
When the first chars differ, the loop breaks at `i=0`, `i > 42` is False, and the trailing identical characters never get skipped
Dozens of them, just sitting there in the diff output
Fix: loop from `i=1` instead of `i=0`:
The existing test `test_text_skipping_trailing` doesn't catch this because both test strings start with `'a'`, so the accidental `left[-0]` comparison happens t

requirementSignalSummary.requirementText: Fixes: https://github.com/nodejs/node/issues/64286
Fix recursive `mkdir` in `node:vfs` so symlinked intermediate
directories are followed, matching native `fs` behavior
The change preserves the caller-facing return value for the first
created path while creating directories in the resolved symlink target
Assisted-by: openai:gpt-5.5
